_description: Post-publish repurposing lifecycle. Tracks published posts and suggests cross-platform repurposing over 14 days. Read by /social and /social-review.

lifecycle_stages.description: Post goes live on its primary platform
If the original was a single tweet or non-Twitter post, expand into a 3-5 tweet thread with more detail
lifecycle_stages.condition: original was well-received (5+ likes) OR was a deep topic

lifecycle_stages.description: Adapt for LinkedIn — add context, make it a mini-story, first-person framing
Adapt for Bluesky — slightly different angle or framing than the tweet
Post to relevant subreddit as a discussion or experience report. Use self-reply linking pattern.
lifecycle_stages.condition: topic is technical enough for Reddit AND hasn't been posted there

lifecycle_stages.description: Quote-tweet your original with a follow-up insight, update, or 'one week later' take
lifecycle_stages.condition: original got engagement OR you have a genuine update

lifecycle_stages.description: If the topic has legs, expand into a Dev.to or Medium article with code examples and deeper analysis
lifecycle_stages.condition: topic generated questions/discussion AND you have enough material for 1000+ words

lifecycle_stages.description: Mark lifecycle complete. Log final engagement metrics. Flag for potential future reference in related posts.

rules.skip_if_flopped: If original got <3 likes after 48 hours, skip the lifecycle — don't repurpose content that didn't resonate
rules.adapt_dont_copy: Every repurposed version must be rewritten for the target platform's voice. Never copy-paste across platforms.
rules.dont_force_it: Skip any stage where you don't have a genuine new angle. Forced repurposing reads as spam.

tracking._note: Active lifecycles are tracked in learnings.json under repurposing.active_lifecycles
